Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Produce Documents From Defendants S O D Home Group, Inc. And Oren Dagan And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Vladie Viltman, Esq. (415.593.7333; vladie@bsoriano.com) of Law Office of Brian E. Soriano for Plaintiff, Nihal Group HK Limited.
Sharice Marootian, Esq. (818.760.2000; sbm@agrlaw.com) of Law Offices of Abdulaziz, Grossbart & Rudman for Defendants, S O D Home Group, Inc., et al.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF NIHAL GROUP HK LIMITED, A FOREIGN COMPANY's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Produce Documents From Defendants S O D Home Group, Inc. And Oren Dagan And For Sanctions is denied as moot. Sanctions granted in the amount of $3,017.50 against defendants.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Oren Dagan
Off Calendar Apr-04-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRIAN E. SORIANO, Esq. of Law Office of Brian E. Soriano (Tel: 415-593-7333) for plaintiff;
Atty. SHARICE B. MAROOTIAN, Esq. of Abdulaziz, Grossbart & Rudman (Tel: 818-760-2000) for defendants S O D Home Group, Inc. and Oren Dagan.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS S O D HOME GROUP, INC. AND OREN DAGAN's Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants S O D Home Group, Inc. and Oren Dagan's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied.

"[T]he party moving to compel arbitration bears the burden of establishing the existence of a valid agreement to arbitrate." (Hotels Nevada v. L.A. Pac. Ctr., Inc. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 754, 758; see also Rosenthal v. Great Western Fin. Securities Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 413.) The arbitration provision is void for failure to comply with Business and Professions Code section 7191, and Defendants therefore fail to meet their burden.

Section 7191, subsection (a) requires the arbitration provision in a contract for work on residential property with four or fewer units to be titled "ARBITRATION OF DISPUTES." The provision here is titled "Arbitration." (Dagan Decl., Ex. A, pp. 27-28.) Section 7191, subsection (a) further requires, "[i]f a provision for arbitration is included in a printed contract, it shall be set out in at least 10-point roman boldface type or in contrasting red print in at least 8-point roman boldface type, and if the provision is included in a typed contract, it shall be set out in capital letters." The parties do not allege whether the contract is "printed" or "typed." Nevertheless, while the arbitration provision is set out in 12-point roman type, paragraphs A, B, and C are not set out in either boldface type or capital letters. (Dagan Decl., Ex. A, pp. 27-28.) The Court does not have discretion to enforce arbitration provisions that fail to comply with section 7191. (Woolls v. Superior Ct. (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 197, 208-212 ["failure to comply with section 7191 renders an arbitration provision per se unenforceable."]; see also Malek v. Blue Cross of California (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 44, 63 [reaffirming Imbler v. PacifiCare of Cal., Inc. (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 567 and holding that strict compliance with Health and Safety Code section 1363.1 is necessary to enforce a health care service plan arbitration provision].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
